meta.title: Lifecycle Convergence — Session Recap
meta.description: 10 verbs, one envelope — the trigger schema that unifies /capture, /close, /sitrep, HUD, next-actions

props.title: 10 verbs, one envelope
props.subtitle: Every lifecycle skill emits ad-hoc shapes. Underneath, one trigger schema is trying to get out.

props.content: This session started with /ws resume burning 40% of an agent's context window and ended with a unifying insight: /capture, /close, /sitrep, HUD, next-actions, flowminds — they're all projections of one stream. What's missing is the stream itself.

items.title: Workstream split approved
items.description: lifecycle-engine (workflow) vs graph-cms (substrate). User: 'I agree.' 0.75 confidence decision filed.

items.title: Ownership boundary formalized
items.description: graph-cms read-only for this agent. Other agent owns substrate. Sole active workstream = lifecycle-engine.

items.title: /ws resume pain surfaced
items.description: 40% context burned reading workstream + N handoffs. Cursor pattern proposed.

items.title: NAAC investigation
items.description: demo-eval = 1011 lines, 64 sessions, 77 handoffs in ONE workstream. Agents never shard.

items.title: Standards rejected
items.description: User: standards are abstract. Behavior must live in skills + ws YAML itself. Agents must shard autonomously.

items.title: Capture + propose reprioritized
items.description: User barely uses /ws. Real drivers are /capture, /close, /handoff. Priority queue flipped.

items.title: Trigger schema convergence
items.description: 10 skills table reveals it: every verb wants the same envelope. /sitrep = projection, /close absorbs /capture, learnings become typed.

items.title: Ship trigger schema v1
items.description: One file defines the envelope. /close + /sitrep migrated first. Rest follow.

props.label: THE CATALOG
props.title: 10 lifecycle verbs — math, metrics, KPIs, gaps
props.subtitle: Each verb carries its own ad-hoc output shape today. The right column is what's missing.

props.label: THE FIX
props.title: Before → after
props.subtitle: 10 ad-hoc shapes collapse to one envelope + N projections

props.title: ⚠ Today
props.content:
**10 verbs** each write their own output shape.

**3 dashboards** (/sitrep, HUD, handoff pbcopy) read **3 different sources**.

**/close** emits prose learnings — bg agents can't apply them without NLP.

**/ws resume** reads workstream + N handoffs + git diff to reconstruct state.

**NAAC proof:** one workstream accumulated 64 sessions, 1011 lines, cursor pointer exists but nobody trusts it.

props.title: ✓ After
props.content:
**1 envelope** — every verb emits `lifecycle_trigger.v1`.

**1 stream** — `.lev/pm/workstreams/<id>/triggers/` — tail it, project it, ship it.

**/close absorbs /capture** — one trigger writes items + learnings + gates + cursor + commit. Nothing in memory by contract.

**/sitrep = projection** — tail the stream, group by cursor.blocker, done. No custom logic.

**Bg agents apply self-learnings** — iterate learnings[*], select by applies_to. Mechanical, no NLP.

props.label: THE ENVELOPE
props.title: lifecycle_trigger.v1 — one schema, ten fillers
props.subtitle: Every verb fills different slots. The shape is constant.

props.content:
schema: lifecycle_trigger.v1
verb: capture | research | interview | propose | exec | close | handoff | work | sitrep | ll
entity_ref: ws://lifecycle-engine | task://... | proposal://...
timestamp: 2026-04-15T02:10:00Z
agent: claude-session-3
commit_before: af0fccf827

# WHAT WAS DONE (structured, typed per-verb)
outputs:
  items: []            # /capture, /research — entities touched
  learnings: []        # /close, /exec — {id, claim, evidence, applies_to, propagates}
  gates: []            # /exec, /close, /interview — {gate_id, score, threshold, pass}
  artifacts: []        # any verb — paths written
  decisions: []        # /propose, /interview — {id, decision, rationale}
  sources: []          # /research — {url, evidence_type, novelty}

# MATH — computed scores this verb's math produced
metrics:
  fidelity: 0.85       # /capture
  ambiguity: 0.16      # /interview
  confidence: 0.78     # /propose
  drift: 0.08          # /close
  novelty: 0.62        # /research

# CURSOR — the only thing /ws resume reads
cursor:
  last_verb: capture
  last_at: 2026-04-15T02:10:00Z
  last_commit: 903ba126e9
  next_verb: propose
  next_input: "ship workstream-as-meta-prompt + beads trial"
  blocker: null

# NEIGHBORS — graph edges to touch
recommend:
  - { entity: ws://graph-cms, verdict: monitor, note: "other agent owns" }
  - { entity: skill://sitrep, verdict: rewrite, note: "duplicates /capture" }

props.label: PROPOSALS FILED
props.title: Three doors to the same room
props.subtitle: Trigger schema is the substrate. Overlays and beads are implementations.

props.title: 🧬 Workstream as meta-prompt
props.content:
**Confidence 0.78.** Workstream YAML carries overlay instructions — agents read them and behave. Portable cross-repo because the instruction lives INSIDE the file, not in a standards/ directory they can't see.

Key blocks: `cursor:`, `overlays:`, `archive_policy:`.

`.lev/pm/proposals/20260415-workstream-as-meta-prompt.yaml`

props.title: 🪢 Beads-backed workstream trial
props.content:
**Confidence 0.72.** One workstream = one beads epic. `lev ws` wraps `bd`. Resume = `bd ready --epic=<id>`. Shard = `bd create --blocks`.

File-based ws was always dogfooding the graph. Graph is ready.

Trial: lifecycle-engine ws, 1 week, rollback if resume-cost stays high.

`.lev/pm/proposals/20260415-ws-beads-trial.yaml`

props.title: ⚡ Lifecycle trigger schema
props.content:
**Emerging — not yet filed as proposal.** The substrate both above proposals implement. Every verb emits `lifecycle_trigger.v1`. Stream becomes source of truth.

Unlocks: /close absorbs /capture · /sitrep becomes projection · bg agents apply learnings mechanically · HUD reads one source.

Next turn: promote this to a formal proposal.

props.label: PAIN POINTS OBSERVED
props.title: Two failure modes, one root cause
props.subtitle: NAAC and Lev break in opposite directions from the same skill design

props.title: NAAC: under-split
props.content:
**1 workstream · 64 sessions · 1011 lines.** 77 handoffs point to demo-eval. 2 sibling workstreams for a multi-week, multi-topic project.

Cursor pointer (line 10) exists but unused — agents read the whole file defensively.

**Root cause:** cost asymmetry. Appending session_N is 1 edit. Sharding is a decision file + new ws + lineage_ref + handoff + 4 commits. Agents take the cheap path 64 times.

props.title: Lev: over-investigate
props.content:
**40% of context window** on /ws resume. Reads workstream.yaml + N handoffs + git diff + asks anchor question before doing real work.

**Root cause:** skill doesn't trust any specific block. No cursor contract. Every resume re-derives state from prose — archaeology instead of reading one pointer.

Same skill runs in lev + naac. Fix lands both repos.

props.label: USER CORRECTIONS
props.title: Four rejections that shaped the design
props.subtitle: Each one killed a false path

rows.proposed: New dna/standards/ file for ws discipline
rows.replaced: Behavior baked into ws YAML itself (overlays)
Agents shard autonomously via overlay trigger

rows.lever: Backfill cursor block in 7 lev workstreams
Levmail NAAC team the overlay pattern

props.label: THE INSIGHT
props.title: One schema. Ten projections. Endless leverage.

props.content: Skills are YAML. FlowMind is YAML. Workstreams are becoming YAML-with-overlays. Now triggers — the events those verbs emit — want the same treatment. Declaration → runtime reads → behavior follows. Same pattern, every layer. That's the meta-substrate Lev keeps rediscovering.
